title: Individual Notification ‚Äî Letter Generation & Mailing Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) if tickets drive mailhouse
spl:
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_notice" earliest=-120d
| eval mailed_epoch=strptime(mailed_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval breach_discovered=strptime(breach_discovered_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval days_to_mail=round((mailed_epoch-breach_discovered)/86400,1)
| where letter_status!="Mailed" OR days_to_mail>60 OR isnull(mail_tracking_id)
| stats count by incident_id, letter_status
| sort - count
description: Tracks generation, print, and certified mail tracking numbers for breach letters within the 60-day window to support individual notification compliance.

grandmaExplanation: We track generation, print, and certified mail tracking numbers for breach letters within the 60-day window to support individual notification compliance ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) if tickets drive mailhouse.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_notice` (`incident_id`, `patient_token`, `letter_status`, `mail_tracking_id`, `mailed_dttm`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Integrate print vendor webhooks; (2) never index full SSN‚Äîuse tokenized IDs; (3) legal sign-off field in same event.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_notice" earliest=-120d
| eval mailed_epoch=strptime(mailed_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval breach_discovered=strptime(breach_discovered_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval days_to_mail=round((mailed_epoch-breach_discovered)/86400,1)
| where letter_status!="Mailed" OR days_to_mail>60 OR isnull(mail_tracking_id)
| stats count by incident_id, letter_status
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Individual Notification ‚Äî Letter Generation & Mailing Evidence (¬ß164.404(b), (d)(1))** ‚Äî Tracks generation, print, and certified mail tracking numbers for breach letters within the 60-day window to support individual notification compliance.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_notice` (`incident_id`, `patient_token`, `letter_status`, `mail_tracking_id`, `mailed_dttm`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) if tickets drive mailhouse. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: hipaa:breach_notice. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="hipaa:breach_notice", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **mailed_epoch** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **breach_discovered** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_mail** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where letter_status!="Mailed" OR days_to_mail>60 OR isnull(mail_tracking_id)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by incident_id, letter_status** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gantt-style timeline (discovered ‚Üí mailed), Table (exceptions).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
